benchmark_scope: Allocation-stage runtime evidence for governed role/resource assignment, policy evaluation, and diagnosis of upstream allocation blind spots.

families.summary: Positive runtime proofs now show that allocation plan, policy evaluation, and resource claim artifacts are carried through the auditable loop in both proof families.
covered_expectations: allocation is represented by first-class runtime artifacts
policy and claim state remain linked to the proof chain

families.summary: Allocation blind spots are no longer invisible to the benchmark surface: diagnosis can name upstream allocation and policy evidence and convert that into a concrete follow-up task.
covered_expectations: allocation mistakes are treated as organizational causes, not only execution mistakes
allocation evidence can generate a runtime follow-up task

remaining_gap: wrong-role-assignment negative families are still not benchmarked as standalone recorded verdicts
allocation coverage remains partial even though benchmark families now exist
